commit.message:
fix: restore responsive layout for markpress build across all screen sizes (#6)

* Initial plan

* fix: restore mobile responsiveness and fix minor issues in markpress build

- Fix require path: use require('markpress') instead of require('./node_modules/markpress')
- Fix typo: 'Extended Feaures Example' → 'Extended Features Example' in presentation.md
- Add mobile responsive CSS (≤900px and ≤700px) to fix viewport clipping:
  - Reduce .bullet-item/.numbered-item font-size and padding on tablets/phones
  - Remove min-height constraint at ≤700px so content determines step height
  - Reduce .lead, .slide-title, .summary, .note sizes at ≤700px
  - Reduces step height from 1183px → 487px at 390×844, eliminating 169px clipping

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/vanduc2514/presentation-agent-harness/sessions/c0796bfa-47aa-47e4-abd8-a7e255ba351e

Co-authored-by: vanduc2514 <52255055+vanduc2514@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add responsive breakpoints for laptop and tablet viewports

- Add @media (max-width: 1600px): set .step { width: 100vw } — eliminates
  the horizontal clipping caused by impress.js returning windowScale=1 while
  the step canvas stays at 1600px (80px clipped at 1440px, 288px at 1024px)
- Add @media (max-width: 1366px): remove min-height, reduce font sizes (title
  4rem, lead 2rem, bullets 1.35rem) and padding so slides fit common 768px
  laptop heights; set step-split-layout height:auto
- Add @media (max-width: 1024px): further scale fonts for tablet landscape
  (title 3.2rem, lead 1.65rem, bullets 1.2rem) and collapse grids to 1 column

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/vanduc2514/presentation-agent-harness/sessions/30cf7e3c-f01d-4e41-ab9b-1aacd17b213b

Co-authored-by: vanduc2514 <52255055+vanduc2514@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: hide split-layout diagram/image at ≤1024px to prevent overflow

At tablet landscape (1024×768), the step-split-layout was collapsing to
single column but the diagram/figure stacked below pushed total content
height beyond the viewport. Fix: hide .hero-image-card and figures inside
.step-split-layout at ≤1024px so only the text content panel renders.

Verified across all breakpoints:
- Desktop 1920×1080: ✓ full layout with images
- Laptop 1440×900: ✓ full layout
- Laptop 1366×768: ✓ reduced fonts, fits
- Tablet landscape 1024×768: ✓ single column, image/diagram hidden, fits
- Tablet portrait 768×1024: ✓ 92vw width, fits (shellH=576)
- Mobile 390×844: ✓ fits (shellH=547)

commit.message:
[ci] B: Resolve latest tag by semver instead of releases/latest

The /releases/latest GitHub API endpoint returns the most recently
published release, not the highest semantic version. When a release
is missing (e.g. v1.5.0 tag exists but no release was created), the
endpoint returns an older version, causing downstream consumers to
be downgraded.

Replace the releases/latest call with a semver-sorted tag lookup so
the workflow always picks the highest vX.Y.Z tag.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[WIP] Fix scrolling issue on desktop for mobile size screens (#4317)

* Initial plan

* fix(dropdown): remove is-clipped-touch on unmount to prevent scroll lock

On desktop browsers with a mobile-sized viewport, when a Dropdown is
destroyed (e.g. SPA route navigation) while its mobile-modal overlay is
open, the `is-clipped-touch` class was left on the html element. This
caused `overflow: hidden` to remain applied at touch viewport widths,
permanently locking page scrolling until a hard reload.

Modal and Sidebar already perform this cleanup in beforeUnmount; apply
the same pattern to Dropdown.

Fixes: Scrolling Fails On Desktop on Mobile Size Screens

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/buefy/buefy/sessions/20c2242c-c915-4c97-b9ef-55721b962540

Co-authored-by: wesdevpro <11604664+wesdevpro@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(docs/navbar): remove scroll lock when hamburger menu is open

TheNavbar toggled `is-clipped-touch` on document.documentElement
whenever the burger menu opened, which applied `overflow: hidden` at
sub-1024px viewport widths and prevented page scrolling while the menu
was active. The navbar menu is an inline CSS toggle (not a full-screen
overlay), so it must never lock page scroll.

Remove the `toggleHtmlClip()` method and its `isMenuActive` watcher.
Add `is-clipped-touch` cleanup to `beforeUnmount` as a safety net in
case of any residual scroll-lock state.

commit.message:
Enforce formatter/linter split: migrate unary-plus-on-identifier semantic rewrite from format to lint (#8323)

* Agent: Target State Alignment

* Enforce formatter/linter split: migrate unary-plus-on-identifier semantic rewrite from format to lint

The formatter was silently removing `+identifier` → `identifier` via `shouldOmitUnaryPlus` in
`format/src/printer/print.ts`. This is a semantic content rewrite, not a layout transform:
the unary `+` operator applies numeric coercion, so `+x` and `x` are not equivalent when
the variable holds a non-numeric value. Formatters must never change program semantics.

Boundary enforced: target-state.md §2.1, §3.2 — "Formatter must not perform semantic/content rewrites."

Changes:
- Remove `shouldOmitUnaryPlus`/`unwrapUnaryPlusCandidate` from `format/src/printer/print.ts`
- Update format test to assert `+count` is preserved verbatim
- Add formatter-boundaries-ownership test for the invariant
- Add new lint rule `gml/no-unary-plus-on-identifier` with autofix in `@gmloop/lint`
- Register rule in catalog, create-gml-rules switch, and rule-contracts stable list
- Add six standalone tests covering autofix, non-identifier preservation, and `++` non-regression

commit.message:
fix: use target disk for scratch on live ISOs with tmpfs /var (#9)

On live-ISO environments /var is backed by tmpfs (~2 GiB), which is far
too small for the multi-gigabyte temporary files that skopeo and
containers/image produce during OCI export. The previous hardcoded
/var/fisherman-tmp path assumed /var was always disk-backed.

Detect the tmpfs case at runtime (statfs TMPFS_MAGIC) and fall back to
a .fisherman-scratch directory on the already-formatted target disk.
A self-bind mount on the scratch directory makes it appear as a mount
point so bootc's empty-rootdir check tolerates it (bootc rejects plain
directories in the target root but allows mount points).

Changes:
- cmd/fisherman/main.go: add isTmpfs() helper, detect tmpfs /var, create
  scratch on target disk with self-bind mount, pass ScratchDir in Options
- internal/install/bootc.go: add ScratchDir field to Options, add
  scratchDir() accessor, replace all hardcoded /var/fisherman-tmp refs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(crawlers): replace naive regex detectLang with trigram-based detection

5 crawlers (Coop, EFG, EOC, LIS, USI) had local detectLang functions using
hand-picked keyword regexes — prone to false positives on mixed-content ATS
pages and misdetection on short text. Replaced all with the shared trigram-
based detectLanguage from detect-language.mjs.

Each crawler preserves its original fallback language:
- Coop, EOC, LIS, USI: fallback 'it' (Italian-dominant portals)
- EFG: fallback 'en' (English-dominant Oracle HCM portal)

This consolidation ensures consistent, accurate language detection across
all 6 dedicated crawlers (SBB was fixed in the previous commit).

commit.message:
fix(sbb-crawler): detect actual source language instead of hardcoding 'it'

Root cause: sourceLocale was hardcoded to 'it' for all jobs.sbb.ch pages,
but Grigioni jobs (Chur, Landquart) are in German. This caused
mergePreserveLocaleData to treat 'it' as the source locale slot, overwriting
existing Italian translations with German-based slugs/titles every crawl run.

Fixes:
- Import trigram-based detectLanguage from detect-language.mjs
- Replace naive regex detectLang with the robust trigram detector
- Defer sourceLocale determination for non-login.org pages until after
  title+description are parsed, then use detectLang() on actual content
- login.org pages (Ticino) still use localizedLoginData-based detection

Affected jobs: Rangierspezialist (Landquart), Lokführer (Chur) — both had
their Italian slugs/titles overwritten with German on every crawler run,
creating an endless churn cycle repaired by translate-pending.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: auto-reconnect on connection drops

Add automatic reconnection with configurable exponential backoff when the
relay connection drops unexpectedly.

New APIs:
- TunnelRelayClient.connectWithReconnect(policy:) — AsyncStream that
  yields a new TunnelRelayStream on each (re)connection
- TunnelRelayClient.onStateChangeHandler — observe all state transitions
  including .reconnecting(attempt:) and .disconnected
- ReconnectPolicy — configurable maxAttempts, initialDelay, maxDelay,
  backoffMultiplier (defaults: 5 attempts, 1-30s exponential)
- TunnelRelayStream.isActive — check if underlying channels are alive

Internals:
- WebSocketBinaryFrameHandler.channelInactive detects connection drops
- Stream.onDisconnect wired to resume the reconnect loop
- CheckedContinuation-based wait/resume for disconnect detection

13 new tests (142 total):
- ReconnectPolicy: exponential backoff, capping, defaults, equality
- RelayConnectionState: .reconnecting equality, .reconnectFailed error
- TunnelRelayClient: onStateChangeHandler, disconnect state propagation
- WebSocketBinaryFrameHandler: channelInactive callback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
